summary: Acetazolamide reduces intraocular pressure through its mechanism involving carbonic anhydrase inhibition but does not significantly alter maximum reaction velocity (\(V_{max}\)). Minor, possibly enduring changes may affect Michaelis constant (\(K_m\)), yet overall function stabilizes after acute phase responding normally eventually despite continued presence at low levels during treatment duration. The focus was primarily on maintaining clarity regarding how enzymes adapt dynamically upon repeated intervention which aligns well within provided options emphasizing stability rather than drastic
